Bonus Skllls: Martial Arts /Gung Fu +1, Language /Any Capellan Secondary +1 Bonus Tralts: Capellans believe strongly in karma and the vagaries of fortune and S0 receive Exceptional Attribute /Edge The player must decide if his character holds Capellan citizen: ship: without citizenship (minimum SOC 3). Capellan charac: ter has the Stigma Servitor Caste Trait and may not purchase (or receive through events) the Owns Vehicle Wealth; Well: Connected or Well-Equipped traits. Path Restrictions: The following paths have a additional prerequisite of Capellan citizenship Capellan  characters: Military School (Stage 21 and all Stage 3 paths plus all Stage paths except Ne'er-Do-Well (P. 48). Child Iabor is lega in most parts of the Confederation; if character cannot qualify for any Stage Stage 3 paths he can go to Stage and enter the workforce, taking Civilian Job and ignoring the prerequisites_
CAPELLAN CONFEDERATION (HOUSE LIAO) The  Capellan Confederation survivor; thanks greatest asset Its people_ Capellan citizen sees it as his or her duly to serve the nation first and himself second; individual rights are therefore subordinate the needs otthe state Regarded as repressive by most other Successor States and smaller   political entities this simple trait has allowed the Capellan nation t0 survive and prosper even after the Federated Commonwealth broke its back in the Fourth Succession War: As always adversity merely strengthens the Capellan Confederation. While may be forbidden to question the state or the author ity of the ruling House Liao_ Capellan citizens enjoy great deal of freedom their personal lives Humankind' various religions and philosophies live together more harmoniously the Confederation than in any other nation. Racia and sexual dis- crimination are virtually unneard caste system exists for those who are not warriors nobility; but it acts more as means of improving the quality of life for each caste rather than as the kind of rigid stratifier that exists among the Clans and in the Draconis Combine. Individuals are encouraged to better them selves and may marry into higher caste or qualify for one on their own merits. The only real bias in the Confederation is favor- able disposition toward those citizens who embrace theif  nation rich cultural heritage_ The Capellan legacy extends back centuries further than that of other Great Houses; having its roots in the ancient heritage of the Han (Chinese people of Terra. Tne Confederation hassolid ties with the Magistracy of Canopus and the Taurian Concordat, the two Periphery states with which it has recently formed the Trinity Alliance. Trade technological aid and mutual military support are the Trinity' $ cornerstones; By contrast; Ioyal Capellans rarely feel anything but  contempt for the Federated Suns and its ruling House Davion_ most recently for ravaging the Confederation during the Fourth Succession War: Other   political relations tend to fall somewhere in between these two extremes Chancellor Sun-Tzu Liao is currently attempting t0 reclaim the worlds of the tny Ives  Compact; which seceded from the Confederation thirty years ago under Sun-Tzu' $ aunt, Duchess Candace Liao_
FREE WORLDS LEAGUE (HOUSE MARIK] Technically tederation rather than true nation the Free Worlds League has been plagued by divisive conflicts through out its history: Until recently; this balkanization greatly limited the League impact on external events_ The current Captain- General , Thomas Marik; war leader with dictatorial powers appointed "for the duration of the crisis_ has done much to centralize power and bring out the nation true strength_ Citizens of the League value their right to express them selves freely, leading to broad acceptance of differing cultures and values along with an appreciation for the arts and educa tion. The League is home to broad spectrum of religions_ but their impact on society as whole has been limited by the gen erally pragmatic League citizenry; who prefer tangible results to the life of the spirit: Business ad the pursuit of material gain, including the recovery of technology: are central to League life_ Ironically, though the League pioneered much of the medical technology used during the Star League era_ social pressures havesince led many to scorn bionics and other replacement technologies, particularly among the lower classes. The  Succession Wars have colored the League relation ships with its neighbors, though the Iate 3050s saw detente with its historical enemies the Capellan Confederation and the Lyran Alliance. While relations remain good with Katherine Steiner- Davion' realm; recent events have  soured relations with the Capellan Confederation. Historically on good terms, the League ties to the Federated Suns were shattered by the formation of the Federated Commonwealth and the Fourth Succession War. The two powers   fought brief 3057 after   Thomas Mank earned that his only son Joshua had died in the care of Davion physicians at the New Avalon Institute of Science
